text.tspan: Separate thread / connection pool per downstream dependency or per tenant.
Libraries: Resilience4j Bulkhead, Hystrix (legacy), Polly (.NET), Failsafe (Java).
USE: most common form; per-dependency thread pools in HTTP clients.
Limit how many in-flight requests for a dependency, without dedicated threads.
Lighter than thread pools (no context switch); only limits concurrency.
USE: async/reactive code where threads are virtual; cheaper isolation.
Split risky workloads into separate processes u2014 a misbehaving one OOMs or crashes
USE: legacy systems where you can't refactor; security isolation.
Separate compute/resources per tenant (or per customer-tier).
Noisy-neighbor tenant can't exhaust resources of others.
USE: multi-tenant SaaS; Kafka quotas; database connection limits per user.
Production segregated from staging; critical from non-critical.
USE: deployment stamps (Microsoft pattern); cell-based architecture (Slack, AWS).
Distinct AZ/regions, with no shared blast radius.
AWS S3 outage in us-east-1 doesn't affect us-west-2 services.
USE: high-availability designs; the standard recommendation from AWS/GCP/Azure.
